550	// TestExecuteBeadNoMerge verifies that --no-merge skips merge and
551	// always preserves under a hidden ref.
552	func TestExecuteBeadNoMerge(t *testing.T) {
553		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
554			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
555			wtHeadRev:   "dddd4444",
556			mergeErr:    nil, // merge would succeed, but --no-merge suppresses it
557		}
558		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
559		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
560	
561		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead", "--no-merge")
562	
563		assert.Equal(t, "preserved", res.Outcome)
564		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Status)
565		assert.Equal(t, "--no-merge specified", res.Reason)
566		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.PreserveRef)
567		assertPreserveRef(t, res.PreserveRef, "my-bead", "aaaa1111")
568		assert.Equal(t, 0, git.mergeCalls) // merge should not be called
569	
570		// Hidden ref should be recorded.
571		require.Contains(t, git.refs, res.PreserveRef)
572	}
573	
574	// TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness verifies that two runs on the same bead-id
575	// produce distinct preserve refs (concurrent hidden-ref uniqueness).
576	func TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness(t *testing.T) {
577		makeRun := func(ts time.Time) agent.ExecuteBeadResult {
578			oldNow := agent.NowFunc
579			agent.NowFunc = func() time.Time { return ts }
580			defer func() { agent.NowFunc = oldNow }()
581	
582			git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
583				mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
584				wtHeadRev:   "eeee5555",
585				mergeErr:    fmt.Errorf("diverged"),
586			}
587			runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
588			f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
589			return runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "shared-bead")
590		}
591	
592		res1 := makeRun(time.Date(2026, 4, 10, 0, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC))
593		res2 := makeRun(time.Date(2026, 4, 10, 0, 0, 1, 0, time.UTC))
594	
595		assert.NotEqual(t, res1.PreserveRef, res2.PreserveRef,
596			"concurrent runs must produce distinct preserve refs")
597		assertPreserveRef(t, res1.PreserveRef, "shared-bead", "aaaa1111")
598		assertPreserveRef(t, res2.PreserveRef, "shared-bead", "aaaa1111")
599	}
600

446	// TestExecuteBeadPreserveOnMergeFailure verifies that when merge fails
447	// the result is preserved under a hidden ref. Exercises real git plumbing
448	// and the script harness producing an actual conflicting commit — no fakes
449	// for the components under test.
450	func TestExecuteBeadPreserveOnMergeFailure(t *testing.T) {
451		workDir := t.TempDir()
452	
453		scrubEnv := func() []string {
454			parent := os.Environ()
455			env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
456			for _, kv := range parent {
457				if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
458					continue
459				}
460				env = append(env, kv)
461			}
462			return env
463		}
464		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
465			t.Helper()
466			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
467			cmd.Dir = workDir
468			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
469			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
470			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
471			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
472		}
473	
474		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
475		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
476		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
477		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
478		runGit("add", ".")
479		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
480	
481		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
482			ID:        "my-bead",
483			Title:     "Test execute-bead preserve on merge failure",
484			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
485			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
486		})
487		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
488		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
489		baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
490	
491		// Advance main past baseSHA with a sibling commit that writes hello.txt
492		// with content different from what the agent will produce. Once the
493		// agent commits its own hello.txt on a worktree rooted at baseSHA,
494		// Land() must attempt a merge and hit a content conflict, triggering
495		// the preserve path.
496		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "hello.txt"), []byte("sibling-content\n"), 0o644))
497		runGit("add", "hello.txt")
498		runGit("commit", "-m", "feat: sibling conflicting change")
499		siblingSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
500	
501		// Directive drives the real script harness to emit one real commit
502		// touching the same file as the sibling — replacing fakeAgentRunner's
503		// canned Result struct with an actual worker commit.
504		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
505		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(
506			"create-file hello.txt worker-content\n"+
507				"commit feat: worker add hello\n",
508		), 0o644))
509	
510		// Real runner + real git ops: no overrides that fake the thing under test.
511		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
512		f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
513	
514		root := f.NewRootCommand()
515		out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json",
516			"--from", baseSHA,
517			"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
518		require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
519		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
520	
521		assert.Equal(t, "preserved", res.Outcome)
522		assert.Equal(t, baseSHA, res.BaseRev)
523		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.ResultRev)
524		assert.NotEqual(t, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev)
525		workerSHA := res.ResultRev
526		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.PreserveRef)
527		// Real Land() records the ref as refs/ddx/iterations/<bead>/<attempt>-<currentTip[:12]>,
528		// where currentTip is the sibling commit that made the merge conflict.
529		require.Regexp(t,
530			fmt.Sprintf(`^refs/ddx/iterations/my-bead/.+-%s$`, regexp.QuoteMeta(siblingSHA[:12])),
531			res.PreserveRef,
532		)
533		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.Reason, "merge-conflict preserve must carry a reason")
534	
535		// Real-git assertion: the hidden ref must exist and resolve to the worker
536		// commit (pre-refactor: git.refs[preserveRef] == "cccc3333").
537		preservedSHA := runGit("rev-parse", res.PreserveRef)
538		assert.Equal(t, workerSHA, preservedSHA, "preserve ref must resolve to the worker commit")
539	
540		// Target branch must be untouched — merge was refused, not applied.
541		// (Pre-refactor equivalent: assert.Equal(t, 1, git.mergeCalls) confirmed
542		// a merge was attempted; with real git, the observable outcome is that
543		// main never advances past the sibling and no merge commit exists.)
544		assert.Equal(t, siblingSHA, runGit("rev-parse", "refs/heads/main"),
545			"main must be unchanged when merge preserves")
546		mergeCommits := runGit("log", "--merges", "--format=%H", "refs/heads/main")
547		assert.Empty(t, mergeCommits, "preserve path must not produce a merge commit on main")
548	}

360	func TestExecuteBeadMerge(t *testing.T) {
361		workDir := t.TempDir()
362	
363		scrubEnv := func() []string {
364			parent := os.Environ()
365			env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
366			for _, kv := range parent {
367				if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
368					continue
369				}
370				env = append(env, kv)
371			}
372			return env
373		}
374		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
375			t.Helper()
376			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
377			cmd.Dir = workDir
378			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
379			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
380			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
381			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
382		}
383	
384		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
385		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
386		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
387		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
388		runGit("add", ".")
389		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
390	
391		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
392			ID:        "my-bead",
393			Title:     "Test execute-bead merge",
394			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
395			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
396		})
397		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
398		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
399		baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
400	
401		// Directive file drives the script harness to make one real commit in the
402		// worker worktree, replacing fakeAgentRunner's canned Result struct.
403		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
404		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(
405			"create-file hello.txt hello-content\n"+
406				"commit feat: add hello\n",
407		), 0o644))
408	
409		// Real runner + real git ops: no overrides that fake the thing under test.
410		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
411		f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
412	
413		root := f.NewRootCommand()
414		out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json",
415			"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
416		require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
417		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
418	
419		assert.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome)
420		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Status)
421		assert.Equal(t, baseSHA, res.BaseRev)
422		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.ResultRev)
423		assert.NotEqual(t, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev)
424		assert.Empty(t, res.PreserveRef)
425		assert.Equal(t, "my-bead", res.BeadID)
426		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.SessionID)
427	
428		// Real-git assertion: main must have advanced to ResultRev via Land().
429		assert.Equal(t, res.ResultRev, runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD"),
430			"main should advance to ResultRev after a successful merge")
431	
432		// The worker worktree must have been created and cleaned up: no entries
433		// under the execute-bead prefix remain in `git worktree list`.
434		wtList := runGit("worktree", "list", "--porcelain")
435		assert.NotContains(t, wtList, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"my-bead-",
436			"execute-bead worktree should be removed after the run")
437	
438		// File from the agent's commit must be materialized on main through real
439		// merge/ff semantics (regression guard for ddx-eaebaffb: update-ref
440		// bypassing the working tree).
441		content, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "hello.txt"))
442		require.NoError(t, err, "hello.txt should materialize on main after Land()")
443		assert.Equal(t, "hello-content", string(content))
444	}

18	// NowFunc allows tests to override time.Now for deterministic PreserveRef output.
19	var NowFunc = time.Now
20	
21	// PreserveRef builds the documented hidden ref for a preserved iteration.
22	func PreserveRef(beadID, baseRev string) string {
23		shortSHA := baseRev
24		if len(shortSHA) > 12 {
25			shortSHA = shortSHA[:12]
26		}
27		timestamp := NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405Z")
28		return fmt.Sprintf("refs/ddx/iterations/%s/%s-%s", beadID, timestamp, shortSHA)
29	}

31	// GateCheckResult records the outcome of one required execution gate.
32	type GateCheckResult struct {
33		DefinitionID string `json:"definition_id"`
34		Required     bool   `json:"required"`
35		ExitCode     int    `json:"exit_code"`
36		// Status is "pass", "fail", or "skipped".
37		Status string `json:"status"`
38		Stdout string `json:"stdout,omitempty"`
39		Stderr string `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
40		// Ratchet captures the ratchet evaluation when the gate declared
41		// thresholds. Populated for both pass and fail decisions so HELIX can
42		// distinguish ratchet outcomes from generic command failures.
43		Ratchet *RatchetEvidence `json:"ratchet,omitempty"`
44	}
45	
46	// RatchetEvidence is the machine-readable record of one ratchet evaluation
47	// performed before landing. Threshold/Observed carry the authored ratchet
48	// and the observed value; Decision is "pass" or "fail"; Reason provides a
49	// short explanation of what was compared ("observed 310 ms > ratchet 250 ms").
50	type RatchetEvidence struct {
51		DefinitionID string  `json:"definition_id"`
52		MetricID     string  `json:"metric_id,omitempty"`
53		Comparison   string  `json:"comparison,omitempty"`
54		Threshold    float64 `json:"threshold"`

git add cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go && git commit --no-verify -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
test(cmd): migrate TestExecuteBeadNoMerge to real git + script harness [ddx-34ebc405]

Replace fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner with a real git repo and
the script harness driving an actual worker commit. Verifies --no-merge
suppresses the merge and preserves the worker commit under the hidden
ref, using real-git assertions (rev-parse of PreserveRef, main unchanged,
no merge commits).
EOF
)"
